title: Marketing Analytics Manager (m/f/d)
required_skills: Attribution modeling (multi-touch, data-driven, Markov chain, Shapley value)
matched_skills: Attribution modelling (explicitly listed in CV skills)
Cohort analysis (listed as 'customer segmentation & cohort analysis')
Looker Studio (listed in tools and experience)
missing_skills: dbt / Dataform — no mention of SQL-based data modeling frameworks
BigQuery / ClickHouse — no cloud data warehouse experience mentioned
GTM (Google Tag Manager) — not listed despite GA4 presence
Incrementality / uplift testing — not mentioned anywhere in CV
LTV modeling — cohort analysis present but LTV not explicit
Probabilistic / statistical modeling (Markov chain, Shapley value) — not evidenced
Advanced SQL / data modeling — SQL listed but no advanced qualifier or modeling depth shown
Python for analytics — listed but no analytical use-case demonstrated in bullets
core_responsibilities: Develop and improve marketing measurement frameworks (multi-touch attribution, incrementality testing, LTV modeling, cohort analysis)
Build and maintain SQL-based data models (dbt / BigQuery) for channel performance and business reporting
Design and evaluate attribution models including probabilistic approaches (Markov chain, Shapley value)
Develop and automate dashboards and performance reports in Looker Studio for key stakeholders
Partner with marketing, media, CRM, and product teams to translate insights into strategic decisions
recommended_emphasis: Lead with attribution modelling — explicitly name multi-touch attribution and any model types used (even rule-based) in CV bullets
Reframe cohort analysis bullets to include LTV framing — e.g. 'cohort-based retention and lifetime value analysis'
Highlight any SQL complexity — reference joins, window functions, CTEs, or data pipeline work to signal advanced SQL capability
Quantify Looker Studio / BI dashboard impact in bullets — audience size, decision cadence, or business outcome driven
Add a brief mention of GA4 in an experience bullet (not just tools list) to show hands-on application
If any A/B or test-and-learn work exists, frame it as incrementality or uplift testing language
Emphasize cross-functional stakeholder management with marketing, media, and product — directly mirrors JD language
Cover letter should address BigQuery/dbt gap honestly but signal fast ramp-up via SQL proficiency and cloud analytics mindset
